reason: Faster Pussycat! Kill! Kill!’s wild go-go girl mayhem and over-the-top thrills epitomize cult B-movie fun
The Blob’s gooey terror and kitschy ’50s vibe deliver classic B-movie camp at its slimiest best
Mystery Science Theater 3000’s riffing on cheesy sci-fi amplifies the joy of wild, campy exploitation cinema
Mars Attacks!’ absurd alien invasion and gleefully campy absurdity celebrate unhinged genre excess
Showgirls’ outrageous Vegas spectacle and camp excess blend so-bad-it’s-good thrills and glamor
Army of Darkness’ cheesy one-liners and slapstick undead battles embrace campy horror-comedy mayhem
The Adventures of Priscilla, Queen of the Desert’s flamboyant humor and outlandish spirit define joyous camp
Bio-Dome’s goofy duo antics and ’90s stoner antics deliver whimsically dumb, guilty-pleasure camp
Barbarella’s kitschy sci-fi sets and Jane Fonda’s playful performance capture sixties camp in a cosmic romp
Austin Powers’ swinging ’60s spy parody revels in groovy camp and over-the-top comedy indulgence
